interpretation: A large train-test gap suggests overfitting, leakage, or unstable learning.
review_question: Is the model performing materially worse on held-out data than on training data?

interpretation: A shifted-test decline warns that ordinary test performance may overstate deployment reliability.
review_question: Does performance decline when the data-generating conditions shift?

interpretation: Feature shift should be documented before generalization claims are made.
review_question: Are future or deployment cases distributed differently from training cases?

interpretation: Even a technically held-out test set can become contaminated if repeatedly used for tuning.
review_question: Was the test set used repeatedly for model selection?
